nativeRange.habitat: Fast-flowing streams and rivers, often attached to submerged rocks and roots near rapids

summary: The African Water Fern (Bolbitis heudelotii) is an epiphytic fern from West and Central African rivers, prized for its finely divided, dark green fronds. It grows attached to driftwood or rock rather than in substrate, and is generally considered an intermediate-difficulty aquascaping plant.
careNotes: The African Water Fern is an epiphytic rhizome fern from West and Central African streams and rivers, where it grows attached to submerged wood and rocks near fast-flowing water. The rhizome (the thick horizontal stem from which the leaves grow) must NEVER be buried in substrate — it will rot. Instead, the plant should be tied to driftwood or porous rock with fishing line, thread, or cyanoacrylate glue until the roots anchor naturally, which typically takes four to six weeks. It is a slow grower that appreciates moderate to brisk flow, which helps it shed debris and acquire nutrients through the water column. Water should be soft to moderately hard and slightly acidic to neutral; the plant has been reported to melt in hard water and dislikes sudden parameter swings. Submerged fronds are typically 15–40 cm tall with finely divided, dark green, somewhat transparent leaves. The species is generally compatible with most community tankmates but should be kept away from fish that uproot or consume plants.
breedingNotes: Propagated vegetatively by cutting the horizontal rhizome into segments, each retaining at least one leaf and several roots, and attaching each segment to a new piece of hardscape. New plantlets also develop directly from mature leaves and can be detached and re-attached once they have a small rhizome and roots of their own. Emersed growth is possible and produces fertile fronds, but submerged-only specimens rarely sporulate in aquaria.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary source for light/CO2/growth-rate/size/placement/propagation. Located via Tropica site search from the supplied search URL.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked placement, propagation (rhizome splitting), rhizome-burial warning, and flow preference. Disagrees with Tropica on lighting (low vs medium) and CO2 (not necessary vs medium); averaged toward medium light and optional CO2.

additional.name: Shrimp and Snail Breeder (aquariumbreeder.com)
additional.notes: Used for temperature range (20–28 °C), pH (6.0–7.5), gH (ideally below 6, tolerant to ~12), epiphytic nature, and melt-in-hard-water caveat.
